commit.message:
Fix pivoting-agent lint: put search_skills/list_skills on same line as "do not"

The lint test checks per-line for search_skills()/list_skills() references
and only exempts lines containing "do not" or "never call". The prohibition
was split across two lines, so the line with the function names didn't match.

commit.message:
Ffix ironplc docs for what functions are supported  (#566)

* Fix standard library docs to reflect actual support status

Many functions and all function blocks were listed as "Not yet supported"
but are actually implemented in the compiler. Updated status for:
- Math functions (ABS, SQRT, LN, LOG, EXP, trig functions)
- Selection functions (SEL, MAX, MIN, LIMIT, MUX)
- Bit shift/rotate functions (SHL, SHR, ROL, ROR)
- Arithmetic operators (ADD, SUB, MUL, DIV, MOD) - removed "(INT)" qualifier
- Comparison operators (GT, GE, EQ, LE, LT, NE) - removed "(INT)" qualifier
- Type conversion functions
- All function blocks (TON, TOF, TP, CTU, CTD, CTUD, R_TRIG, F_TRIG, SR, RS)

String functions remain marked as not yet supported.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Nk2YL9CeuccMp6Z9KjgcaL

* Revert function block status back to not yet supported

Function blocks (TON, TOF, TP, CTU, CTD, CTUD, R_TRIG, F_TRIG, SR, RS)
are not actually supported yet.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Nk2YL9CeuccMp6Z9KjgcaL

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: distributed photo sharing system for system design learning

Implements a production-grade photo sharing system demonstrating:
- PostgreSQL table partitioning (time-based sharding, 7-year archival)
- Redis cache-aside pattern (LRU, TTL, hit/miss metrics)
- Elasticsearch inverted index (full-text search, dual-write)
- MinIO 3-tier object storage (HOT/WARM/COLD, presigned URLs)
- FastAPI stateless backend (horizontally scalable)
- Storage lifecycle migration worker (60% cost savings at 1 PB scale)
- Interactive capacity calculator for interview prep

Scale: 500M users · 2M photos/day · ~1 PB · 7-year retention · 200ms SLA

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: photo-system
repository.description: Photo sharing system to learn system design — PostgreSQL partitioning, Redis cache-aside, Elasticsearch, MinIO storage tiering, 500M user scale

commit.message:
fix(04-02): fix frontend notification matching and persistence

- Extract archibaldOrderId from JOB_COMPLETED result payload
  and set it as jobOrderId on the pending order
- Cache completed jobId→orderId mapping in ref for lookup
  after fetchPendingOrders resets state from server
- Cache verification notifications in ref so they survive
  server refetches (server doesn't store jobOrderId)
- VERIFICATION_RESULT handler now matches via completedOrderIdsRef
  when jobOrderId isn't set yet on the order
- Enrich orders from server response with cached jobOrderId
  and verificationNotification before setting state

Root cause: pending_orders table has no job columns;
jobOrderId/jobStatus are ephemeral frontend state that gets lost
on every fetchPendingOrders() call from server.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[en] Translate carnets 042-047 (44 entries, Sep-Oct 1875)

6 carnets covering Marie's Audiffret obsession: the grandfather's vulgar
commentary on Gioia (045), anonymous letters in block capitals (045),
the Alice de Nevers premiere at the Nice theatre (047), the proto-feminist
"learn from the infamous sex" meditation (047), and the martyr's crown
fashioned from rose thorns (047).

Team: 3 translators + editor + conductor. Session died mid-run;
ED completed 5 remaining entries and set approval flags on resumption.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: bashkirtseff
repository.description: Complete uncensored diary of Marie Bashkirtseff (1858–1884) — 105 notebooks, censored by her mother for 140 years. Full original texts, AI-assisted translation pipeline, historical research, multilingual ebook/PWA.

fix: re-sign binary on macOS after auto-update and rollback

`fs::copy` invalidates the ad-hoc Mach-O code signature, causing
Apple System Policy to SIGKILL the binary on launch. Run `codesign
--sign - --force` after replacing the binary in both `download_and_install`
and `rollback`.

Fix: Populate columnsArray for environment variable case matching

Fixed: "error: Fix the env variable clientid/clientsecret/etc"

The columnsArray was empty because it was only populated inside the
setupEnvironments() function that was deleted. The fixCase() function
depends on columnsArray to convert lowercase attribute names back to
their correct camelCase form for input field IDs.

Now columnsArray is populated on page load with all environment variable
names in their correct camelCase format, allowing fixCase() to properly
match lowercase attribute names to camelCase input field IDs.

commit.message:
feat(admin-app): admin dashboard with prompt editor and agent tester (#18)

## Summary

- **Admin auth**: Email/password login via Supabase; users without
`is_admin = true` in `profiles` are rejected at login
- **Prompt editor**: Browse and edit per-language markdown files
(`web-api/agent/tutor/languages/*.md`) with a split editor/preview pane
and save button
- **Test Agent**: Chat interface with full LLM response inspector —
shows `raw_responses`, `messages`, and `usage` from each `Runner.run()`
call for rapid prompt iteration
- **Backend**: New `/admin/*` routes protected by `admin_auth`
dependency; admin chat sessions track state in-memory using the real
admin user's ID
- **Database**: Two new migrations — `create_profiles_table` (with
auto-create trigger) and `add_admin_role` (`is_admin boolean DEFAULT
false`)
- **Render**: Admin app added to blueprint as a static site service
- **CLAUDE.md**: Supabase safety rules added (no `db reset` or
production push without explicit permission)

## Granting admin access

```sql
UPDATE profiles SET is_admin = true
WHERE id = (SELECT id FROM auth.users WHERE email = 'your@email.com');
```

## Test plan

- [ ] Apply migrations locally: `supabase migration up`
- [ ] Log in with an admin account — should succeed
- [ ] Log in with a non-admin account — should show "Access denied" and
sign out
- [ ] Navigate to Prompts, edit a language file, save, verify file
changed on disk
- [ ] Navigate to Test Agent, send a message, verify full LLM JSON
appears in right panel
- [ ] Verify Render deploy picks up the new `arabic-voice-agent-admin`
static site service

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix gradient death: add skip connection and LayerNorm to MACHIterative

The gradient chain test_loss → patches → basis → action_head →
transformer → GRU → error_proj was too deep. GRU saturated at
max_abs=0.999, killing all gradient to error_proj (exactly 0.0).

Two fixes:
1. LayerNorm after error_proj: prevents large inputs from saturating GRU
2. Skip connection: error_obs passed directly to action_head
   (obs_conditioned=True), creating short gradient path that bypasses
   transformer and GRU entirely

Also reverted per-component LR groups (the 10x boost accelerated
GRU saturation rather than helping).

  payment GL warning, CSV export on transactions, balance sheet & P&L fixes,
  pending sales orders filter fix, invoice detail page

- Bug fixes: silent GL failures, dead export buttons, cross-module data consistency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: remove racing keepalive, add reconnect-and-retry on query failure

Removed the setInterval keepalive which ran conn.execute() outside the
query queue, causing race conditions with concurrent queries. Replaced
with a reconnect-once-and-retry pattern inside executeQuery so dropped
sessions (idle timeout, network reset, warehouse auto-resume) are
recovered transparently without surfacing errors to the user.

commit.message:
Comprehensive audit: fix critical bugs, expand tests, improve all skills

- Fix detect_divergence.py: add lower_is_better param to prevent false
  alarms on accuracy/higher-is-better metrics
- Fix implement_utils.py: preserve directory structure in backups to
  prevent filename collisions
- Improve result_analyzer.py: add Spearman correlation, status filtering,
  zero-baseline epsilon guard
- Improve gpu_check.py: memory-aware availability (not just utilization)
- Improve parse_logs.py: add Python logging and tqdm format support
- Improve experiment_setup.py: PID tracking, regex-filtered exp IDs,
  stale experiment cleanup
- Add pipeline_state.py: phase validation, state save/load, resumption
- Add schema_validator.py: contract validation for pipeline data
- Add plot_results.py: ASCII chart visualization for reports
- Update all 9 skills with bug fixes and improvements
- Update all 4 agents with missing tools and enhanced workflows
- Update all 6 reference docs with new patterns and schemas
- Add 6 new test files (contracts, error handling, agents, pipeline,
  schema, plotting) — 158 tests pass
- Add 5 realistic test fixtures (noisy, OOM, partial, tqdm, logging)
- Expand evals from 12 to 20 cases

32 files changed, +923/-81 lines

commit.message:
Claude/fix GitHub sync games etn xg (#40)

* fix: use NBA CDN scoreboard for game sync and improve fallback logic

The sync-games script relied on PBPStats /live/games/nba which only
returns the current day's games. After PBPStats rolls over to the new
day (around midnight ET), yesterday's completed games disappear from
the endpoint, causing the daily sync at 05:30 UTC to find no games.

Changes:
- sync-games.ts: Use NBA CDN todaysScoreboard as the game list source
  (always available, includes game date), then fetch detailed stats
  from PBPStats per game ID (which still works for past games)
- useTodayGames: When PBPStats returns empty (no games today), fall
  back to showing the latest Supabase completed games regardless of
  date, instead of requiring an exact date match with today
- GamesView: Show the actual game date in the header when displaying
  yesterday's games, and exclude displayed games from historical list

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UyGU2XbxSxwRUy3ebWRTAX

* fix(sync): replace PBPStats with NBA CDN for game logs sync

Switch from per-entity PBPStats API calls (~500+ requests) to
NBA CDN boxscore endpoint (~10-15 requests per day).

- Incremental sync: only fetch new completed games from scoreboard
- Each boxscore contains all player + team stats in one response
- Backfill mode (--backfill) to re-sync from game_stats
- NBA CDN is public and doesn't block cloud/CI IPs

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UyGU2XbxSxwRUy3ebWRTAX

---------

commit.message:
feat(transform): hybrid evaluator for node_modules (#772)

* feat(transform): hybrid evaluator that shakes ESM node_modules

Instead of ignoring all node_modules during evaluation, the new hybrid
evaluator detects CJS vs ESM and only ignores CJS files. ESM packages
in node_modules are now properly shaken, allowing their exported values
to be resolved during VM evaluation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add changefile for @griffel/transform

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use path.extname instead of manual string slicing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: use sourceType unambiguous + experimentalLazy for CJS/ESM detection

Uses oxc-parser's lazy deserialization to skip AST materialization when
only module metadata is needed. Falls back to standard parsing on
platforms that don't support raw transfer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restructure ESM fixture to avoid node_modules gitignore issue

Use a relative import (./esm-dep) instead of a node_modules directory
that gets excluded by .gitignore in CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: verify shaker receives correct arguments in hybrid evaluator tests

Use toHaveBeenCalledWith to assert all four arguments are forwarded.
Replace bare {} casts with a proper MOCK_OPTIONS object.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: remove redundant ESM integration test and fixture

The hybrid evaluator is fully covered by unit tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: use single hybrid evaluator rule instead of two

The hybrid evaluator already handles both CJS (ignore) and ESM (shake),
so there's no need for a separate shaker rule and node_modules rule.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add node_modules check in hybrid evaluator

Non-node_modules files go straight to shaker without CJS/ESM detection
overhead. Only node_modules files are checked. Tests restructured with
node_modules cases under a single parent describe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  qualifier prefix preserved: '(Map 2) Oxuji Esports vs. Team Novaq'
  instead of the verbose '(Map 2) Will Oxuji Esports win in the ... match'
- Suppress series ticker (KXCS2MAP etc.) in the in-game market list embed
- Apply mid-price to group preview odds in build_embed (was still using ask-only)
- 'Select a threshold to bet' → 'Select an outcome to bet'

fix(agent-service): upgrade Azure OpenAI API version for json_schema support (#10)

`response_format: json_schema` requires API version 2024-08-01-preview or later.
